recap:
# Match Recap

England survived a major scare against Congo DR in the round of 32, falling behind to Brian Cipenga's early goal before Harry Kane's second-half brace secured a 2-1 comeback victory at the Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta. Congo DR's goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi made several crucial saves to keep his team in the contest, while England's shaky defensive display—particularly from centre-backs pulled out of position early on—created repeated openings that nearly proved catastrophic. Anthony Gordon's introduction off the bench proved decisive, setting up Kane twice as England managed to weather a relentless second-half assault to book a quarter-final meeting with Mexico.
